articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.name: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

article.headline: Portugal survive late scare in friendly win over Finland
article.description: Manchester United midfielder Bruno Fernandes came off the bench to score twice and help Portugal kick off their Euro 2024 preparation with a 4-2 win over non-qualifiers Finland on Tuesday in Lisbon.
article.linkText: Ronaldo out as Fernandes lead Portugal to win

article.story:
Manchester United midfielder Bruno Fernandes came off the bench to score twice and help Portugal kick off their Euro 2024 preparation with a 4-2 win over non-qualifiers Finland on Tuesday in Lisbon.

Portugal were without captain Cristiano Ronaldo, who is resting before reporting for duty on Friday ahead of his sixth European Championship, so manager Roberto Martinez fielded a highly changed squad which dominated from the start.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

Defender Ruben Dias gave the hosts the lead with a stooping header from a corner in the 17th minute and Diogo Jota extended their lead from the spot after Francisco Conceicao was fouled inside the area in stoppage time before the break.

Fernandes, who was one of five substitutions at half-time, netted Portugal's third with a fine curling shot into the upper corner from the edge of the box in the 55th minute.

Finland substitute Teemu Pukki took advantage of a complacent Portugal defence to score twice within five minutes to bring his side back into the game.

However, Fernandes took charge and secured the win with a close-range strike in the 84th minute.

Portugal face Croatia on Saturday and Ireland next week in their final warm-up games before they start their European Championship campaign on June 18 against Czech Republic in Group F. They will also take on Turkey and Georgia.

keyEvents.text: Goal!  Portugal 1, Finland 0. Rúben Dias (Portugal) header from the right side of the six yard box to the top right corner. Assisted by Vitinha with a cross   following a corner.
Goal!  Portugal 2, Finland 0. Diogo Jota (Portugal) converts the penalty with a right footed shot to the bottom left corner.
Substitution, Finland. Pyry Soiri replaces Nikolai Alho.
Substitution, Portugal. Bruno Fernandes replaces João Palhinha.
Substitution, Portugal. Diogo Dalot replaces Nuno Mendes.
Substitution, Portugal. Gonçalo Inácio replaces Rúben Dias.
Substitution, Portugal. Gonçalo Ramos replaces Diogo Jota.
Substitution, Portugal. Pedro Neto replaces Rafael Leão.

Substitution, Finland. Casper Terho replaces Oliver Antman.
Substitution, Finland. Teemu Pukki replaces Benjamin Källman.
Substitution, Finland. Robin Lod replaces Urho Nissilä.

Substitution, Portugal. Danilo Pereira replaces António Silva.
Goal!  Portugal 3, Finland 2. Teemu Pukki (Finland) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Matti Peltola with a through ball.
Substitution, Finland. Leo Walta replaces Anssi Suhonen.
Goal!  Portugal 4, Finland 2. Bruno Fernandes (Portugal) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Francisco Conceição.
